skill.name: wordpress-woocommerce-development
skill.description: Workflow guidance for planning and building WooCommerce stores, including setup, products, payments, shipping, customization, testing, and optimization.
skill.summary: Plan WooCommerce store setup, payments, shipping, customization, testing, and optimization workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown inline code, fenced prompts, and ordinary words in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found; residual risk is low because the workflow delegates to other skills that may perform implementation work.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The detected Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code or fenced prompt examples that name other skills. They are not executable code and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited locations are Markdown list items or fenced text prompts. No source file contains Ruby code, shell execution syntax, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Overmatched Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections at the cited lines match ordinary prose or skill names, not cryptographic API usage. No MD5, DES, SHA1, or similar weak algorithm implementation was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML description text and line 107 is a Markdown skill reference. The context contains no hashing, encryption, or credential handling behavior.

low_findings.title: Delegates Work to External Skill Names
low_findings.description: The workflow repeatedly instructs users to invoke other skills for setup, payments, shipping, customization, testing, and optimization. This is normal workflow behavior, but the safety of those delegated skills must be evaluated separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dependency references are explicit and broad, but this file does not execute them automatically. Risk depends on the behavior of the referenced skills at use time.

content.value_statement: WooCommerce projects require coordinated setup across products, payments, shipping, design, and testing. This skill provides a phased workflow that helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code guide implementation tasks.
actual_capabilities: Breaks WooCommerce store delivery into setup, product, payment, shipping, customization, extension, optimization, and testing phases.
Lists concrete actions for store settings, tax rules, product attributes, variable products, and product images.
Guides payment gateway planning for Stripe, PayPal, offline payments, and test checkout flows.
Covers shipping zones, shipping methods, flat rates, free shipping, and carrier integration planning.
Defines quality gates for product display, checkout, payments, shipping, email, and mobile responsiveness.
Provides copy-ready prompts for coordinating with related implementation and testing skills.
limitations: It is a workflow document, not an executable WooCommerce plugin or theme.
It does not include source code for payment gateways, subscriptions, bookings, or custom product types.
It depends on separate skills or developer work for implementation, testing, and security review.
It does not validate live store configuration, payment credentials, tax compliance, or carrier rates.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Store Build
use_cases.description: Use the phased checklist to scope WooCommerce setup, product data, payment methods, shipping, and launch testing.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Ecommerce Implementation
use_cases.description: Assign store setup, design, payment, optimization, and testing work to the right specialists or AI tools.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Store Launch Review
use_cases.description: Use the quality gates to verify checkout, payment processing, shipping calculation, email delivery, and mobile behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Store Setup Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this WooCommerce workflow to create a setup plan for a new store. Include store settings, taxes, currency, product categories, and launch checks.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Products and Shipping
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan WooCommerce product configuration and shipping. Cover categories, attributes, variable products, shipping zones, flat rates, and free shipping rules.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Payments and Checkout Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to define a WooCommerce payment integration plan. Include Stripe, PayPal, offline payments, checkout tests, email notifications, and mobile checkout validation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Launch Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this WooCommerce workflow to create a full launch plan for a subscription store. Include setup, custom templates, extensions, performance optimization, automated tests, and quality gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a small WooCommerce store for physical products.
output: A phased checklist covering setup, products, payments, shipping, customization, optimization, and testing.
A launch readiness list for product display, checkout, payment capture, shipping calculation, email delivery, and mobile layout.

output_examples.input: Prepare payment and shipping tasks for an agency handoff.
output: A task breakdown for Stripe, PayPal, offline payments, shipping zones, flat rates, free shipping, and carrier integration.
Suggested prompts for payment, shipping, testing, and performance specialists.

output_examples.input: Review a WooCommerce store before launch.
output: A concise quality gate review focused on checkout flow, payment processing, shipping calculation, emails, and mobile responsiveness.
A list of missing implementation or testing tasks that require separate tools or manual verification.

best_practices: Use the workflow as a planning checklist before writing theme, plugin, or payment integration code.
Verify payment, tax, shipping, and email behavior in a staging environment before launch.
Review any delegated skill or generated implementation separately before using it on a production store.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the workflow as a complete WooCommerce implementation.
Do not enter live payment credentials into prompts or untrusted tools.
Do not skip checkout, shipping, email, and mobile tests before a store launch.

faq.question: Does this skill create a WooCommerce store automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a phased workflow and prompts for planning and coordinating WooCommerce development work.

faq.question: Can it implement Stripe or PayPal directly?
faq.answer: No. It identifies payment integration tasks and suggests related prompts, but implementation requires separate tooling or developer work.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for existing stores?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide audits, optimization plans, extension setup, checkout testing, and launch readiness reviews.

faq.question: Does it include security testing?
faq.answer: It references testing and WordPress-related workflows, but it does not perform security testing by itself.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as a planning and coordination guide.

faq.question: What should I verify manually?
faq.answer: Verify taxes, legal compliance, payment credentials, refunds, shipping rates, email delivery, and production checkout behavior.
